metadata.notes: agy 1.0.10 has NO manual /compact command (live: /comp → No matches; the binary /compact string is dead). Compaction is AUTO-only; its CHECKPOINT "# Resuming from a compaction" trace is parser-SKIPPED, so compaction is never a distinct observable event. The recipe drives /context (the real context-management slash, no-LLM, no transcript line) to stand in for the context-management window; the asserted visible arc ready→working→ready on each side + the no-waiting compact_window invariant are produced. wait_turn + keys (Escape to dismiss the /context overlay) ported; recorded live.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** agy ships `/compact` (binary strings: `/compact`, `/Compact`, `CompactionInfo`, `GetCompactionInfo`). Real evidence of a compaction event is in sample `9ea5200c` step 4: a `SYSTEM`/`CHECKPOINT` line whose content begins `# Resuming from a compaction` — the resume marker the runtime writes after summarizing history.

**daemon=full, caveat: compaction is INVISIBLE as a distinct event.** The scenario (per the claudecode sibling) asserts only the visible state arc — `ready → working → ready` around `/compact`, then a normal turn afterward to prove the session continues. The antigravity parser deliberately SKIPS all `SYSTEM` lines, including `CHECKPOINT` (`parser.go`: the `default` arm sets `ev.Skip = true` for SYSTEM/CONVERSATION_HISTORY/CHECKPOINT). So the compaction's only transcript trace produces NO state event — there is no manual-compact signal and no `CompactionState`. Whether `/compact` even drives a working→ready arc depends on whether the runtime writes a `PLANNER_RESPONSE` summarization turn around it; the sample shows compaction landing mid-conversation as a bare CHECKPOINT with surrounding normal `PLANNER_RESPONSE`/tool lines, so the user-visible arc is just the normal turn lifecycle on each side. The scenario explicitly accepts that the compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is not observable. Both bracketing turns settle `ready` via the empty terminal `PLANNER_RESPONSE`, satisfying the asserted arc. CAVEAT (emission): no sample drives `/compact` interactively, so whether agy emits a working span for the compaction summarization itself is unconfirmed — `record` settles it. This keeps `daemon=full` (the asserted visible arc IS produced) with the caveat that compaction is not separately observable.
caveats: Compaction is NOT observable as a distinct event: the only trace is a SYSTEM/CHECKPOINT '# Resuming from a compaction' line which the parser SKIPS by design. The dashboard cannot distinguish compaction from a normal turn (same as claudecode — CompactionState is dead code). The spec asserts only the visible state arc.

Emission UNCONFIRMED: no sample drives /compact interactively; whether the summarization itself produces a working span is unverified — record settles whether the compact_window briefly enters working.
Tokens/cost not in JSONL — observations assert cost/tokens = 0.

OBSERVABILITY CORRECTION (live record 2026-06-20): agy 1.0.10 has no manual /compact command — the slash menu (39 cmds) has no compact/summarize trigger; /comp → No matches. Compaction is auto-only and its CHECKPOINT trace is parser-skipped. The recipe substitutes /context (a real no-LLM context-management slash) which produces no transcript line and leaves the session in ready, faithfully capturing the cells observability claim (compaction interactions are non-events; state holds ready; session continues). daemon=full unchanged (the asserted visible arc IS produced); agent_supports=yes unchanged (agy compacts automatically).

sources.note: strings: /compact, CompactionInfo, GetCompactionInfo — the compaction slash command + protobuf surface
step 4 = SYSTEM/CHECKPOINT content '# Resuming from a compaction' — the only compaction trace; parser skips it
default arm sets ev.Skip=true for SYSTEM lines (CONVERSATION_HISTORY, CHECKPOINT) — no compaction state event

preconditions: agy CLI >=1.0.10 on PATH, authenticated via Google OAuth
driver wait_turn seam ported (slash is already elicited)
setup: Fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/; agy mints its own <conv-id>

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: still-here

verify: after /compact the session settles back to ready (no stick in waiting)
post-compaction prompt: ready→working→ready — proves the session continues
